rag_rules: Fix AGREE syntax errors like missing EOF by ensuring proper block termination and valid identifier usage within annex scopes.
When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are visible in the current AADL scope and avoid introducing undefined references.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to prevent type resolution errors.
Validate that AGREE scalar type names match expected int, real, and bool types to avoid resolution failures.
Place implementation-specific assignments in implementation annexes, not component type annexes.
Ensure all AGREE guarantees reference only visible inputs and outputs at the appropriate scope level.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within component type or implementation annexes.
AGREE constants and equations must use valid AADL data types (int, real, bool) and visible identifiers.
anti_patterns: Do not reference implementation-only subcomponents or features in component type annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the input model or its dependencies.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes for assumptions and guarantees
